  return <MDXLayout {...layoutProps} {...props} components={components} mdxType="MDXLayout">


    <h1 {...{
      "id": "concepts"
    }}>{`Concepts`}</h1>
    <p>{`In order to help you get familiar with MetaFox platform more conveniently, this document will explain to you some main platform concepts. The implementation guide won't be covered at this document.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "user-vs-content"
    }}>{`User vs Content`}</h2>
    <p>{`Each blog, video, photo, .... has a detail page. User can comment, share, like, ... on detail pages of those item.`}</p>
    <p>{`Friends list, saved items, report item, ... has no detail pages. Users can not comment on, share or like these items.`}</p>
    <p>{`User, pages, groups have profile pages, activity streams, privacy settings, and owner of other blogs, videos, ... etc`}</p>
    <p>{`To abstract types of theme into programing concepts, we categorize them into `}<strong parentName="p">{`Content`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`User`}</strong></p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "content"
    }}>{`Content`}</h3>
    <p>{`The object will be categorized as `}<strong parentName="p">{`Content`}</strong>{` if:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Have unique id in their types`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Has its privacy value`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Have a detail page`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Can like, comment, share`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`For example: blogs, videos, photos are `}<strong parentName="p">{`Content`}</strong></p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "user"
    }}>{`User`}</h3>
    <p>{`The object will be categorized as `}<strong parentName="p">{`User`}</strong>{` if:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Have a profile page`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Can have others content (blogs, videos, ...)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Have their activity stream`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`For example: users, pages, groups as `}<strong parentName="p">{`User`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Thus, we can call a page, group as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`page`}</inlineCode>{` user, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`group`}</inlineCode>{` user.`}</p>
    <p>{`And, obviously, we can say that `}<strong parentName="p">{`User* is `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`child`}</inlineCode>{` of `}</strong>{`Content*`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "membership"
    }}>{`Membership`}</h2>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`membership`}</strong>{` is relationship of two `}<strong parentName="p">{`User`}</strong>{`. For example:`}</p>
    <p>{`After logged in, a user can:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Send friend request to become friends of others.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Assign his friends to a his friend lists.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Send member request to become group member.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Like a page to become members of page.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`When logged in as group user, a user can:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Assign a members to be group admin, etc`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`We call these relationships are membership.`}</p>
    <p>{`A User has the following `}<strong parentName="p">{`memberships`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`his friends`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`his friend lists`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`A `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`group`}</inlineCode>{` User has the following memberships:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`group members`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`group admins`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`A `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`page`}</inlineCode>{` User has the following memberships`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`page members`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`page admins`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "privacy"
    }}>{`Privacy`}</h2>
    <p>{`When users post items ... on their profile pages, they can set who can see their posts with their memberships`}</p>
    <p>{`For example: A user can post a blog and set the privacy with their `}<strong parentName="p">{`memberships`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`friends`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Specific friend lists`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`In order to keep design cleaned, `}<strong parentName="p">{`privacy`}</strong>{` should be centralized to a place, so it's have a global unique ID.
Then we can map `}<strong parentName="p">{`membership`}</strong>{` to `}<strong parentName="p">{`privacy`}</strong>{` concept as simple as possible.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "action-entity"
    }}>{`Action Entity`}</h2>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`ActionEntity`}</strong>{` is Models which defines who did the action on a Content (can be Content or User - since User was a child of Content).`}</p>
    <p>{`For example: when users do something, for example: like, share, friend request v.v..., Those Models are called `}<strong parentName="p">{`ActionEntity`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "role--permissions"
    }}>{`Role & Permissions`}</h2>
    <p>{`Think `}<strong parentName="p">{`role`}</strong>{` as `}<strong parentName="p">{`user_group`}</strong>{` in phpFox V4`}</p>
    <p>{`Think `}<strong parentName="p">{`permissions`}</strong>{` as `}<strong parentName="p">{`user_group_params`}</strong>{` in phpFox V4. Has two type:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Permission with boolean value`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Permission with actual value`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "policy"
    }}>{`Policy`}</h2>
    <p>{`When user posts a comment, writes a blogs, shares a content ... platform has to verify `}<strong parentName="p">{`role`}</strong>{` & `}<strong parentName="p">{`permission`}</strong>{` to see if the user is allowed to perform such actions`}</p>
    <p>{`When a user posts on others profile pages, platform also have to check `}<strong parentName="p">{`membership`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`privacy`}</strong>{` of the profile owner.`}</p>
    <p>{`To reduce duplicated and keep code cleaned, in platform we will centralize all checking of `}<strong parentName="p">{`permission`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`privacy`}</strong>{` to `}<strong parentName="p">{`Policy`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`In Policy, every action we have 5 privacy layers:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Role/Permission`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`$user->hasPermissionTo('resource.view'); // return bool.
`}</code></pre>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Privacy Owner`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`use MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\Facades\\PrivacyPolicy;

PrivacyPolicy::checkPermissionOwner($user, $owner); // return bool.
`}</code></pre>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`User Privacy Setting of Owner`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`use MetaFox\\User\\Support\\Facades\\UserPrivacy;

UserPrivacy::hasAccess($user->entityId(), $owner->entityId(), 'blog.view_browse_blogs'); // return bool.
`}</code></pre>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Resource Setting: consider to use it on which level depends on context.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`use MetaFox\\Platform\\Facades\\Settings;

Settings::get('blog.privacy_enabled'); // return mixed (your module is free to define var type).

`}</code></pre>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Privacy Resource`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`use MetaFox\\Platform\\Support\\Facades\\PrivacyPolicy;

PrivacyPolicy::checkPermission($user, $resource); // return bool.
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "event"
    }}>{`Event`}</h2>
    <p>{`Laravel's `}<strong parentName="p">{`Event`}</strong>{` is a implementation of `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Observer_pattern"
      }}>{`Observer Pattern`}</a></p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Event`}</strong>{` is simple way to decouple package dependencies and extends platform features. It can be thought as a replacement of MetaFox plugins.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-event-to-notify-an-action"
    }}>{`Using Event to notify an action`}</h3>
    <p>{`When a new user is registered, we can notify an action `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_created`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`$user`}</inlineCode>{` data.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`events()->dispatch('user_created', $user);
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`All listeners registered to the action `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_registered`}</inlineCode>{` will handle the action as below`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class UserCreatedListener
{
  public function handle($user){
      // put your code here
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-event-to-interact"
    }}>{`Using Event to interact`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`$response = event()->dispatch('get_package_settings');
`}</code></pre>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class PackageSettingListener
{
  function handle()
  {
    return ['add_new_setting'=>['database_down']];
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-event-to-call-apis-of-other-apps"
    }}>{`Using Event to call APIs of other Apps`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`event()->dispatch('create_new_feed', $action);
`}</code></pre>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class CreateFeedActionHandler
{
  public function handle($action)
  {
      $feedService->createAction($action);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "model-observer"
    }}>{`Model Observer`}</h2>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Model Observer`}</strong>{` is the integration of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Eloquent`}</inlineCode>{` Model and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Event`}</strong>{` system with events of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`creating`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`created`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updating`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updated`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`deleting`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`deleted`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`class PostObserver
{

  public function creating($post)
  {
    // this method is invoked automatically when Blog is created
  }


  public function created($post)
  {
    // this method is invoked automatically when Blog is created
  }

  // others
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "feed--stream"
    }}>{`Feed & Stream`}</h2>
    <p>{`When a user post blogs, video, photo, ... platform will centralize these actions into feed, and other users can see and interact with.`}</p>
    <p>{`To keep feed in as simple as possible, the platform will organize feed item as below:`}</p>
    <p>{`Each `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`feed`}</inlineCode>{` contains info of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`item_type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`item_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`type_id`}</inlineCode>{`
Note:
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`item_type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`item_id`}</inlineCode>{`: specify the item mentioned about
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`user_id`}</inlineCode>{`: specific `}<strong parentName="p">{`User`}</strong>{` who is created item
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_id`}</inlineCode>{`: specific `}<strong parentName="p">{`User`}</strong>{` where item is posted to (other users, on a page, on a group v.v...)
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`type_id`}</inlineCode>{`: to categorize feed, feed abilities: `}<strong parentName="p">{`can_comment`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`can_like`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`can_share`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`can_edit`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`can_create_feed`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`can_put_stream`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "when-a-feed-is-created-how-to-deliver-it-"
    }}>{`When a feed is created, how to deliver it ?`}</h3>
    <p>{`The trouble comes when feed owner set privacy to many friend lists. We can not keep many `}<strong parentName="p">{`Privacy`}</strong>{` in single feed record, So we put `}<strong parentName="p">{`Feed`}</strong>{` + `}<strong parentName="p">{`Privacy`}</strong>{` to other `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`feed_stream`}</inlineCode>{` tables. It has info of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`feed_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_id`}</inlineCode>{` so we can deliver feed in flexible way.
Note:
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`privacy_id`}</inlineCode>{`: specify who can see
`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`owner_id`}</inlineCode>{`: specify profile page`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "storage--disk"
    }}>{`Storage & Disk`}</h2>
    <p>{`We have `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`core_storage_services`}</inlineCode>{` : currently we support `}<strong parentName="p">{`AWS S3`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Local`}</strong>{`. For each storage service, we can have multiple `}<strong parentName="p">{`Config`}</strong>{` instances (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`core_storage`}</inlineCode>{`).`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Config in config/filesystems.php`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "scope"
    }}>{`Scope`}</h2>
    <p>{`When you want to add custom query to Query Builder, use `}<strong parentName="p">{`Scope`}</strong>{`. MetaFox supports:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://laravel.com/docs/8.x/eloquent#global-scopes"
        }}>{`Global scopes`}</a>{`: this way is not recommended because it will add to Model static and apply for the whole request process.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://laravel.com/docs/8.x/eloquent#local-scopes"
        }}>{`Local scopes`}</a>{`: written inside Model, but it isn't recommended to write too much in Model, should only create `}<strong parentName="li">{`Scope`}</strong>{` that will always be used.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "how-to-apply-scope"
    }}>{`How to apply Scope:`}</h3>
    <p>{`When we build query, we can create a `}<strong parentName="p">{`Scope`}</strong>{` and apply it into current Query.`}</p>
    <p>{`Example:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`
$exampleScope = new ExampleScope();
$exampleScope->setSomeAttribute('123');

$model = new Model();
$query = $model->newQuery()->applyScope($exampleScope);
$data = $query->get();

`}</code></pre>

    </MDXLayout>;
